rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AIKO ONODERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Appointment Of Temporary Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine whether to refer the case to the San Francisco Public Administrator. 2) At the last hearing the minutes say if a Petition for Appointment of a Temporary Successor Trustee is not filed by the next calling of this matter, the court will strongly consider appointing the San Francisco Public Administrator. The court set a status of appointment of temporary successor trustee. M. Armon Cooper tried twice to file a petition to appoint a successor trustee in this estate case when it needs to be filed in the related trust case number PTR-19-303150 Aiko Onodera Living Trust. 3) Court to determine whether to proceed on the petition for probate of lost will and for letters of administration with will annexed and whether probate code 6124 presumption of revocation has been overcome.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/13/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Liabilities need to be on a separate schedule in accordance with Probate Code 1063(g) not as part of the assets on hand at the beginning of the account. (See page 2 account in Supplement). A "revised account" must be filed as an amendment or amended petition and re-noticed. (CRC 7.3, 7.53). 2) Revised entries on disbursements must be filed as an amendment or amended petition and re-noticed. (CRC 7.3, 7.53). An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/29/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEWEL DAVIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/07/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must file and give notice of the petition on the mandatory judicial council form.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN COLIN CAMPBELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/7/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain why notice was sent to George N. Buffington, esq. electronically when there does not appear to be consent to electronic service filed. 2) Please file receipts and invoices for all reimbursements paid to Lolly Sangster in the account. 3) The Will distributed all tangible property to nieces Christina Roman Lee, Shirlee Roman Niel, and Lolly Roman Sanster. Please explain why only a receipt was filed 3/16/2022 by Lolly Roman Saanster. Please file receipts signed by Christina Roman Lee and Shirlee Roman Neil. 4) Please explain and file receipts for the 50k transferred to Lolly Sangster on 6/24/2021 and 50k transferred on 07/27/2021. 5) Please explain and file receipts for 8/4/21 Donovan James Msc. For $150 and $425.00. 6) The court cannot make a determination on liabilities when all liabilities are not known and appear to be estimated. 7) It appears Executor outsourced his duty as Executor in managing estate Ranch operations to Lolly and Executor statutory fees should be reduced by what is sought and has been paid to Lolly. 8) Its appears Executor breached his duty by failing to keep all assets protected by the $250k FDIC limit. 9) Please explain how the court can award fees to Buffington & Aaron, ALC when they have not joined in the petition to request a portion of fees. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/22/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE BARBARA J BARDARO REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions: Appiontment Of Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The guardian ad litem and his counsel must also appear. 1) All the persons named as successor trustees in the 2015 amendment to the 1995 Trust have filed their declination to serve as trustee. The petitioner, who was removed as successor trustee by the 2015 amendment, requests her appointment as successor trustee of the 1995 Trust. The petitioner and her descendants are beneficiaries entitled to distribution of income and principal of the trust for limited purposes at the trustee's discretion. 2) Should a bond be required as a condition of the petitioner's appointment as successor trustee? Probate Code section 15602(a)(3) provides that a trustee is not required to give a bond, "unless an individual who is not named as a trustee in the trust instrument is appointed as a trustee by the court." Subdivison (b) provides that the court may not excuse the requirement of a bond except under compelling circumstances, and for purposes of section 15602, a request by all the adult beneficiaries of a trust that bond be waived is deemed to constitute a compelling circumstance. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF OLEG SLININ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/6/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The court cannot grant distribution based upon information and belief the court needs definitive statements. See page 6 lines 4-5. 2) Please explain how the court can grant distribution to a trustee of a trust that does not appear to be finalized/executed. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/22/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE KENSTOWICZ-LEDWIDGE FAMILY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 4, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Designating Property To Be An Asset Of The Living Trust
rulings.Rulings: continued to 9/6/22. counsel has still not provided or uploaded a proposed order, and has not provided a file stamped hard copy of the petition. please note, as discussed with counsel, the order should reference only the accounts listed in the petition. in addition, the court would like a declaration from the GAL appointed in this case re his evaluation of the petition and potential impact on the minor. finally, copies of the bank statements that cover the date the trust was signed must be filed to show the accounts on hand as of the date of that signing, as the accounts are not identified by number on Schedule A of the trust. deadline for curing all issues is noon on August 29, 2022. please note, examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/11/22 at Petitioner's request. Courtesy copies of all filings and a proposed order must be received by the assigned examiner no later than 9/30/22 to avoid another continuance. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7, and 14.59 E.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOSEPH MUSCAT SEPARATE PROPERTY TRUST OF 2005
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine The Validity Of The February 25, 2019 Amendment And Restatement Of The Joseph Muscat Separate Property Trust Of 2005, For Accounting, And For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse Under The Welfare And Institution Code; For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/20/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must give and file notice. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/6/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.
